Name: LatencyAwarePACBayesOptimization
Title: Latency-Aware PAC-Bayes Optimization for Real-Time Edge Inference
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that a novel PAC-Bayes framework, integrated with latency-aware optimization, can significantly improve the accuracy-latency tradeoff in real-time edge inference tasks, while maintaining computational tractability.
Related Work: Related work has explored various techniques for latency-critical inference, including model compression (Martínez et al., 2025), task-oriented computation offloading (Li et al., 2025), and dynamic model partitioning (Li et al., 2025). However, these approaches often lack a principled theoretical foundation for balancing accuracy and latency. PAC-Bayes theory (Warrell et al., 2022) provides a strong theoretical framework for generalization bounds, but its application to latency-critical tasks remains underexplored. This proposal aims to bridge this gap by developing a latency-aware PAC-Bayes optimization framework.
Abstract: This proposal introduces a novel framework for latency-aware PAC-Bayes optimization, specifically designed for real-time edge inference tasks. By integrating PAC-Bayes generalization bounds with latency constraints, we aim to optimize the accuracy-latency tradeoff in a computationally tractable manner. Our approach leverages Jacobian-based layer-skipping and adaptive thresholding to minimize hidden-state perturbation, ensuring strict wall-clock budgets. We validate our framework through experiments on resource-constrained devices, demonstrating superior performance compared to existing methods.

Experiments.Description: Evaluate the performance of our latency-aware PAC-Bayes optimization framework on a set of real-world edge inference tasks, including object detection and text generation, on ARM and RISC-V platforms.
Experiments.Method: Implement the proposed framework on edge devices and compare it against state-of-the-art methods (e.g., Doppeladler, LAB) in terms of accuracy and latency. Use metrics such as F1-score, inference latency, and energy consumption.

Experiments.Description: Conduct ablation studies to isolate the impact of different components of our framework, such as the PAC-Bayes bounds, Jacobian-based layer-skipping, and adaptive thresholding.
Experiments.Method: Systematically disable and enable each component and measure the changes in accuracy and latency. Analyze the tradeoffs and identify the most effective configurations.

Experiments.Description: Assess the robustness of our framework under varying computational and memory constraints.
Experiments.Method: Vary the computational resources and memory available on the edge devices and evaluate the framework's ability to maintain performance. Use a range of resource-constrained scenarios to test the robustness.

Risk Factors and Limitations: The computational complexity of PAC-Bayes optimization may pose challenges in real-time settings, requiring careful engineering to ensure tractability.
The effectiveness of the proposed framework may vary across different edge devices and tasks, necessitating extensive validation across a diverse set of scenarios.
The tradeoff between accuracy and latency may not always be optimal, and finding the right balance may require fine-tuning of parameters.
